game_name: Tiny Toon Adventures 2: Trouble in Wackyland

danger_signals.visual_form: Roderick Rat and antagonistic characters from show appearing on ride stages
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat/obstacle phase; player must avoid or overcome enemy interference

danger_signals.visual_form: Ride-specific dangers (log ride features Sweetie as pursuer, bumper car/train/coaster obstacles)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental and character-based threats unique to each attraction area

danger_signals.visual_form: Transition to Funhouse stage where 'Secret Admirer' identity will be revealed
danger_signals.meaning: Escalation to final confrontation; accumulated ticket cost signals player readiness threshold

safety_signals.visual_form: Points converted to tickets during ride completion
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward safety goal; enough tickets = access to Funhouse = advancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Each stage features a different protagonist (Plucky, Hamton, Babs, Furrball, Buster)
safety_signals.meaning: Familiar character presence = safe protagonist context; variety reduces monotony threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Montana Land/Wackyland theme park environment with carnival/fair aesthetic
safety_signals.meaning: Established safe recreational space despite hidden threat (Montana Max's scheme)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Specific character appearance (Buster, Babs, Hamton, Plucky, Furrball)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: None documented; characters appear only as playable agents
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ride vehicles match character personality (bumper car=Plucky's chaotic style, train=Hamton's methodical nature, rollercoaster=Babs' daring)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Roderick Rat; other villainous Tiny Toon show characters making cameo appearances
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appear as ride-stage obstacles and interference agents
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed across ride stages to create localized opposition

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Revealed as 'Secret Admirer' persona; amusement park ownership/branding
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Montana Land naming (Japanese version) signals ownership; Wackyland theming (international) obscures true architect

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limited ride access; player must complete available rides to accumulate tickets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Restricted stage selection; grinding mechanic forces repeated ride attempts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasing ticket pool displayed; ride variety increases as more areas become accessible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player gains choice in stage order; obstacle difficulty escalates per ride

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Funhouse entrance becomes accessible when ticket threshold met; distinct visual separation from carnival rides
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced progression to Funhouse stage; no further ride selection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Funhouse environment with obstacles; Montana Max appears as boss entity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat/avoidance sequence against antagonist; narrative twist (friendly inviter revealed as schemer)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title screen logo redesign (Japan: simple title; US/EU: Buster + Babs featured); Map screen park naming (Montana Land vs. Wackyland); textbox repositioning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No gameplay change; purely presentational localization reflecting regional character familiarity

environmental_storytelling.example: Plucky's bumper car matches his reckless personality; Hamton's train reflects his more cautious, methodical nature; Babs' rollercoaster suits her daring adventurousness
Setting established as safe recreational space masks Montana Max's scheme; carnival invitation narrative hides antagonistic intent
Progression from individual ride stages to centralized Funhouse; ticket system gates access to final reveal location
Additional Tiny Toon show characters appearing throughout rides grounds world in established cartoon IP universe; reinforces stakes through familiar character network
Japanese version explicitly names Montana Land (villain's ownership clear); international version uses Wackyland (obscures true location origin, creating mystery)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Interference on rides; create stage-specific obstacles requiring avoidance or defeat
Chases player on log ride; specific threat requiring evasion within ride vehicle constraints
Operates anonymously through amusement park construct; invites player under false pretense
Appears as Funhouse boss; shifts from off-stage schemer to direct combat opponent

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Playable characters (Buster, Babs, Hamton, Plucky, Furrball)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Each character performs ride-specific actions (driving bumper car, piloting train, riding coaster, evading log ride hazards, exploring funhouse); behavior locked to assigned ride context

notes: This game communicates world-state primarily through mechanical progression (ticket system) and environmental theming rather than dynamic visual state changes. Danger is localized to individual ride stages via stage-specific antagonists rather than global world threats. Faction identity is character-based (Tiny Toons vs. villains) rather than ideological. The core manifestation mechanic is the deception framework: amusement park as safe space hosting a hidden scheme. Regional localization creates different narrative clarity (Japan: transparent ownership; International: mysterious setting). Limited visual documentation available; behavioral tells inferred from gameplay structure and character assignments.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor article on Tiny Toon Adventures 2
wikipedia - Tiny Toon Adventures 2: Trouble in Wackyland article
